political.s: Ralph Lauren Corp reported minimal political contributions of $60,058 in 2024 with no federal lobbying. Personal donations by founder Ralph Lauren historically lean Democratic, with most contributions going to Democratic candidates and causes since 1982.

charity.s: Ralph Lauren Corporate Foundation donated $13.4 million in FY25. Primary focus on cancer care and prevention through the Pink Pony Fund, community support, and emergency relief. Significant commitments include $25 million to expand five cancer centers across America and support for HIV/AIDS initiatives.
charity.focus: Cancer care, community development, emergency relief, HIV/AIDS support

environment.s: Ralph Lauren achieved 34% absolute GHG emissions reduction ahead of 2030 target and 100% renewable electricity in owned facilities by FY25. Committed to Timeless by Design 2030 strategy with science-based targets. Good On You rates environmental practices as 'It's a Start' due to progress on materials but gaps in hazardous chemical elimination.

labor.s: Company has comprehensive human rights and labor policies addressing wage equity, forced labor prevention, and worker safety. However, faces ongoing scrutiny: investigated by Canadian authorities for alleged Uyghur forced labor in supply chain; historically involved in Saipan sweatshop case; rated 'It's a Start' by Good On You for supply chain traceability limitations.

dei.s: Ralph Lauren has explicit pro-DEI commitments with dedicated Office of Diversity & Inclusion. Goals include 20% underrepresented minorities in global leadership and gender parity at VP level and above. Glassdoor rates DEI 4/5 stars equal to retail sector average, though Comparably shows it ranks in bottom 35% for diversity perception.

animals.s: Ralph Lauren tests fragrances on animals when required by law and allows third-party testing. Not cruelty-free certified. Company has animal welfare policy for sourcing materials but does not meet strict cruelty-free standards. Parent company L'Oreal conducts animal testing.

guns.s: Not applicable. Ralph Lauren is a fashion and lifestyle products company that does not manufacture, sell, or distribute firearms.

privacy.s: Ralph Lauren collects extensive personal data for e-commerce, marketing, and store analytics including Wi-Fi tracking for occupancy measurement. Privacy policy provides data collection disclosure but uses standard practices for large retailers with third-party sharing for service providers.

execPay.s: CEO Patrice Louvet earned $23.1 million in FY25 (up 39% from prior year) with 91% variable compensation. Executive Chairman Ralph Lauren earned $24.2 million (up 23%). Pay heavily tied to stock performance and company metrics. CEO-to-median-worker ratio not disclosed in available sources.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
